pregnancy_recommendations.recommendation: Discontinue all lipid-lowering medications immediately and avoid using any other lipid-lowering drugs during pregnancy. Consider alternative options for managing cholesterol levels, such as colesevelam, if breastfeeding.

further_actions.recommendation: Offer cascade screening to all first-degree relatives of the patient with familial hypercholesterolemia. This includes siblings and children who are at risk for heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia.

Due to concerns about potential teratogenic effects, close monitoring of the patient's cardiovascular health during pregnancy is recommended. Regular follow-ups with a cardiologist and obstetrician are advised.

title: What Pediatric Endocrinologists Need to Know About Dyslipidemia
